source.title: Documented seed: trust terminal page
source.source_note: This extraction is reconstructed from the documented OpenClaw style lessons and validated slide patterns, not from the original slide screenshot.

claim: Trust should feel concrete through visible runtime control, not abstract reassurance.
visual_anchor: A right-side terminal or control panel acts as the primary trust object.

composition.structure: Left-side trust claims and governance labels paired with a right-side terminal hero object.
composition.alignment_logic: Terminal object sits inside a fixed right frame; trust claims on the left share one vertical grid.
composition.asymmetry: The page is intentionally right-heavy because the terminal carries the authority of the argument.
composition.image_usage: A terminal window or control panel hero visual is required to make trust tangible.
highlight_grammar: accent color on trust badges and runtime indicators
monospace treatment inside the terminal object
subtle glow or frame emphasis only around the terminal

components.position_note: Dominant right-side terminal or control panel object
Small supporting indicators around the trust argument

why_it_works: The terminal turns trust into something inspectable and concrete.
The composition gives the abstract argument a visible runtime object.
The page reads as governance and execution, not as generic marketing reassurance.
anti_patterns: A terminal that is too small to anchor the page.
Dense left text that competes with the visual object.
Decorative indicators that clutter the terminal area.

do_not_copy_blindly: Specific terminal commands or source screenshots
adaptation_notes: Keep the object-centric trust framing even if the terminal becomes a control console.
Use realistic runtime or policy details to preserve credibility.
